uplink.description: New-sensor VOC frame (0x12): lux + 3-byte bVOC (ppb). Two measurements (newest last) plus trailing battery-percentage byte. Synthetic in-bounds input; wire values cross-checked against the upstream TTN decoder (reference/upstream-codec.js parseVOC isNew=true): temp 27.40/27.38 C, humidity 35.5/33 %, pressure 1004.48/1004.45 hPa, lux 120/130, voc(bVOC) 12345/23456 ppb, battery 88 %. Newest reading at top level, older in history.
uplink._note: Synthetic frame hand-encoded per the Enginko data_frame_format (date 4 LSB, temp 2 LSB signed x100, humidity x2, pressure 3 LSB x100, lux 2 LSB, bVOC 3 LSB) and confirmed by running reference/upstream-codec.js as the wire-value oracle. expected.data is our own normalization (bVOC -> air.tvoc; lux -> air.lightIntensity; battery % -> batteryPercent).

uplink.description: Old-sensor VOC frame (0x0C): lux + 2-byte IAQ index. Two measurements (newest last) plus trailing battery-percentage byte. Synthetic in-bounds input; wire values cross-checked against the upstream TTN decoder (reference/upstream-codec.js parseVOC isNew=false): temp 27.40/27.38 C, humidity 35.5/33 %, pressure 1004.48/1004.45 hPa, lux 200/210, voc(IAQ) 150/160, battery 77 %.
uplink._note: Synthetic frame hand-encoded per the Enginko data_frame_format (2-byte IAQ width for the old sensor). Wire values confirmed via reference/upstream-codec.js; expected.data is our own normalization (IAQ -> air.iaq; lux -> air.lightIntensity; battery % -> batteryPercent).

uplink.description: TER frame (0x04): temperature/humidity/pressure only, no lux/VOC. Three measurements (newest last), no trailing battery byte. Input reused from the Enginko data-frame-format worked example (also used by the mcf-lw12co2 sibling). Newest reading at top level, two older in history (newest-first).
uplink._note: TER input from the Enginko data_frame_format worked example. Upstream parseTER assumes exactly three measurements + battery and reads past short buffers (producing null temperatures); we read only the complete records present, so this exact-length 3-measurement frame is the apples-to-apples oracle. expected.data is our own normalization.

uplink.description: Unsupported frame id (0x09 metering) is rejected.
